text: When working with APIs, you typically make HTTP requests to a server and receive data back in JSON format. Authentication is often required, which may involve API keys or access tokens. Rate limits control how many requests you can make within a certain time period, so it's important to handle these by checking response headers. Error handling is also key—you should always check the status code of the response and handle issues like 404 Not Found or 429 Too Many Requests gracefully. By following these principles, you can build reliable integrations with external services.
